title: Native UI parity: framework primitives blocking React-app visual fidelity (Spectr#28 driver)
body:
## Context

Spectr's editor is a 1.86MB React app shipped today via Pulp's WebView. spectr#28 is porting it to render natively through the WidgetBridge (no embedded browser engine, no DOM). This issue is the umbrella for the Pulp framework primitives that block visual parity.

## Methodology — how this list was produced

Built two reusable tools (sibling to @pulp/react, both general-purpose for any future Pulp/React app, not Spectr-specific):

1. **`@pulp/css-adapt`** — `/tmp/pulp-react/packages/pulp-css-adapt/` — CSS-prop → bridge-call adapter. Handles shorthand expansion (padding/margin/flex/border), color parsing (rgb/rgba/hsl/hex/oklch via colord), length parsing, effect-lowering registrations, registerProperty/registerShorthand extension API. 100 vitest tests passing. ESM + CJS + types, `npm pack` → tarball.

2. **`pulp-css-analyze`** — `/Users/danielraffel/Code/spectr/native-react/tools/pulp-css-analyze/` — AOT analyzer that AST-walks any JS bundle (`@babel/parser`), extracts every inline `style={{...}}` object literal, cross-references with Pulp's bridge surface, emits a Markdown coverage report. Optional `--ai` flag for LLM-suggested mappings on unmapped props.

3. **`extract-html-bundle`** — `/Users/danielraffel/Code/spectr/native-react/tools/extract-html-bundle/` — Decodes the original 1.86MB HTML's `<script type=\"__bundler/template\">` JSON-encoded HTML, lifts `<style>` blocks → tokens.json (25 CSS custom properties × 4 themes) + classnames.json (.mono, .tnum), lifts `<script>` blocks → main.js (161KB original Claude Design React code). Static, deterministic, AI-free.

Pipeline:
```
1.86MB HTML → extract-html-bundle → tokens.json + classnames.json + main.js
                                              ↓
React JSX → @pulp/react reconciler → dom-adapter (resolves var() + className)
                                              ↓
                                     @pulp/css-adapt
                                              ↓
                                     bridge setX calls
                                              ↓
                                     Pulp WidgetBridge → Skia/Yoga/Dawn
```

## Coverage report (run on Spectr's `editor.js`)

[Full report](https://github.com/danielraffel/spectr/blob/feature/native-react-editor/planning/spectr-style-coverage-report.md). Summary:

| Category | Count | % |
|---|---|---|
| Style objects parsed | 160 | — |
| Unique CSS properties used | 60 | — |
| **Mapped to bridge** | **43** | **72%** |
| Intentionally dropped (cursor, transition, …) | 9 | 15% |
| Unmapped (silently dropped) | 8 | 13% |

The 8 unmapped properties (38 total occurrences):

| Prop | × | Category | Sub-issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| `backdropFilter` | 11 | Pulp framework gap | #926 |
| `boxShadow` | 8 | Pulp framework gap | #925 |
| `accentColor` | 3 | Adapter gap (form controls only) | open if needed |
| `verticalAlign` | 3 | Adapter gap | open if needed |
| `resize` | 1 | Adapter gap | open if needed |
| `textOverflow` | 1 | Adapter gap | open if needed |
| `touchAction` | 1 | Adapter gap | open if needed |
| `transform` | 1 | Pulp framework gap | #930 |

## Empirical conclusion

**~50% of unmapped occurrences (boxShadow + backdropFilter) require Pulp framework primitives that have no current bridge equivalent.** No amount of adapter polish closes them.

Beyond pure CSS coverage, three additional framework gaps emerged from running the pipeline:

- **Label widget doesn't honor font properties** (#927) — `setFontFamily`/`setFontWeight`/`setLetterSpacing` calls fire but rendering looks identical. Highest visual impact item — typography is the difference between \"this looks right\" and \"this looks placeholder.\"
- **Label widget doesn't auto-grow to fit text** (#928) — fixed-width truncation (`ZOOMABLE FILTER BANK` → `ZOOMABLE FII`).
- **Canvas widget surface not visible through parent** (#929) — `canvas2d` calls log finite coordinates after our rAF/NaN fixes, but the FilterBank center renders white. Likely opaque-default background or layer-compositing issue.

## Assumptions (so we can revise as the prototype reveals more)

1. **The visual parity gap is dominated by framework primitives, not adapter coverage.** Verified by running the pipeline end-to-end: `var()` resolution, className merge, shorthand expansion all work correctly and the screenshot is unchanged from before those landed. CSS coverage is at 72% and the chrome still doesn't look like WebView.

2. **The 6 sub-issues here cover \"most of\" the framework gap, not all of it.** Prototyping each will likely uncover smaller follow-on gaps (e.g. font feature settings #927-companion, canvas blend mode, rRect optimization). Those will be added as additional sub-issues hanging off this umbrella.

3. **macOS-only is acceptable for v0.** Spectr's branch will prototype each primitive locally on macOS arm64 (ar-swap into the SDK's `libpulp-view.a` for fast iteration) and update each sub-issue with findings + draft patch link before the upstream PR opens.

4. **AI is build-time, not runtime.** The analyzer's `--ai` mode generates static mapping suggestions checked into the repo; runtime stays deterministic.

## How this issue evolves

As prototyping each sub-issue reveals new gaps:
- New sub-issue filed
- Linked here under \"Sub-issues to file\" or a new \"Discovered during prototype\" section
- Coverage report re-run on the bundle and updated in this issue's body when totals shift materially

## Sub-issues

### Closed earlier (do not re-pick)
- [x] #925 setBoxShadow primitive on View
- [x] #926 setBackdropFilter primitive on View
- [x] #927 Label widget honors font properties
- [x] #928 Label widget auto-grows to fit text content
- [x] #929 Canvas widget transparent default + draw-surface visibility
- [x] #930 setTransform affine matrix on View
- [x] #932 Register external/fonts/*.ttf with SkFontMgr at startup

### Open (active — auto-picked-up)

**P0 — blocking**
- [ ] #964 FilterBank canvas2D draw output not reaching visible surface (was "child Views opaque-white over canvas" — premise updated 2026-04-29 with empirical proof; framework-side bug, not Spectr)
- [x] #967 View widget default background should be transparent (closed by #973 contract tests, in v0.61.0)
- [x] #992 -[PulpView mouseUp:] SIGSEGV on every click — closed by #1001 (in v0.62.0)
- [ ] #1006 real clicks don't dispatch React onClick handlers (post-#1001 follow-up; filed 2026-04-29; blocks ALL interaction)
- [ ] #998 v0.61.0+ layout regression — position:absolute; inset:0 no longer fills parent (App root shrinks to bottom strip; promoted to P0 — ALL Claude-Design apps use this layout pattern)


**P1 — additive primitives + paint correctness**
- [x] #965 Standalone SVG-path widget — closed via #991 (in v0.61.0)
- [ ] #994 @pulp/react SvgPath intrinsic — wires #991 widget through React JSX (follow-up to #991)
- [ ] #966 Range-slider widget for HTML `<input type="range">` mapping (Spectr workaround `8f1df47` in place)
- [x] #972 View::paint_all() / overflow-visible — closed by #996 (in v0.62.0). Spectr workarounds (5fc9052 + 75e0407) disabled.

**Generalization (CLI / cross-consumer)**
- [ ] #995 import-design CLI should emit a buildable React project, not just ui.js (web-compat shims + dom-adapter scaffold + bundle build + pulp design check)

**P2 — quality-of-life**
- [ ] #968 canvasRect should fall back to active set_fill_color when no color arg
- [ ] #969 CSS-style typography inheritance (parent View → child Label)

### Recently merged PRs
- #973 test(view): lock in transparent View+CanvasWidget contract (merged 2026-04-29 16:38Z, included in v0.61.0)
- #991 feat(view): SvgPathWidget for inline SVG <path> icons (merged 2026-04-29 17:04Z, included in v0.61.0)

## References

- Spectr branch: https://github.com/danielraffel/spectr/tree/feature/native-react-editor
- Coverage report: https://github.com/danielraffel/spectr/blob/feature/native-react-editor/planning/spectr-style-coverage-report.md
- @pulp/css-adapt sibling package
- pulp-css-analyze CLI (general-purpose)
- extract-html-bundle CLI (general-purpose)

title: [SYMPP-V2-SEC-001] Harden child worker handoff output
body:
#### Context

Architect child dispatch previously serialized child worker grant secrets in MCP responses. This keeps child bootstrapping on a private handoff path while preserving standalone create-work private handoff behavior.

#### TL;DR

*Child worker minting returns non-secret handoff metadata, validates handoff options before minting, and reports superseded cleanup status explicitly.*

#### Summary

- Store child worker secrets through `SecretHandoff` and return `secret_handoff` metadata only.
- Key the backing handoff secret target/path by stable grant id while keeping `display_key` in names for readability.
- Persist non-secret cleanup metadata (`mode`, `path`, `target`) for the originally published handoff location.
- Use stable metadata filenames based on work package/grant identity, not repo root or database path strings.
- Keep explicit `metadata_dir` handoffs discoverable by mirroring metadata to the default private metadata store and deleting recorded mirror paths later.
- Retain narrow fallback reads for metadata written by earlier unmerged PR iterations with repo/database-scoped filenames.
- Reject malformed `secret_handoff.mode`, `store_dir`, and `claimed_by` values before minting; supplied `mode` must exactly match a supported canonical mode.
- Continue scanning later metadata mirrors when an earlier metadata copy is unreadable or invalid; valid custom-store metadata still wins by path order.
- Reject child handoff publication with an explicit non-secret configuration error when no verified current-platform handoff helper repo root exists.
- Record metadata mirror files in private handoff metadata so later cleanup can remove default and custom-store metadata mirrors without original `store_dir` opts.
- Mirror custom-store metadata to the default private store and fail new handoff publication if required metadata writes fail.
- Treat superseded grants with missing handoff metadata as nonfatal `no_metadata` cleanup status.
- Return successful new mints with non-secret superseded cleanup warnings when old cleanup fails after commit.
- Keep child grant supersession, minting, and handoff publication atomic with rollback cleanup for failed new handoffs.
- Assert MCP structured/text output omits secret fields, work-key-shaped values, and claim commands.
- Update child-dispatch docs and plugin README with private-handoff guidance.

#### Architect Classification

- Rejected T2 Alpha P1 `Keep handoff storage namespaced by repo/database` as invalid/out-of-contract for PR #52. Do not reintroduce `repo_root` or database-path strings into target/path keys; that conflicts with the accepted stability invariant across worktrees and equivalent database path strings. The uniqueness boundary is work package id plus grant id; `display_key` is readable decoration only.
- Accepted Alpha P2 `Reject malformed secret_handoff field values before minting`; fixed narrowly with pre-mint validation for supplied optional handoff fields and supported `mode` values.
- Accepted Bravo P2 `Metadata mirror cleanup stops after one unreadable copy`; fixed narrowly by continuing metadata lookup across candidate mirrors and returning an error only when no valid metadata can be read.
- Accepted current T2 `metadata_dir` discoverability; fixed narrowly by mirroring explicit metadata_dir handoff metadata to the default private metadata store and cleaning recorded mirror paths.
- Accepted current T2 platform helper check; fixed narrowly so a repo root is valid when it contains the current platform helper, without requiring both Windows and POSIX helpers.

#### Alternatives

- Keep raw response secrets: rejected because MCP output can land in logs and prompts.
- Add grant-schema handoff fields: rejected for this slice; non-secret local metadata is enough without a migration.
- Change standalone create-work handoff contract: rejected because private-store handoff remains supported and exact local target names are not a pre-production compatibility contract.

#### Test Plan

- [x] `mix test test/symphony_elixir/symphony_plus_plus/secret_handoff_test.exs:314 test/symphony_elixir/symphony_plus_plus/mcp_test.exs:3936 test/symphony_elixir/symphony_plus_plus/mcp_test.exs:4234 test/symphony_elixir/symphony_plus_plus/mcp_test.exs:4266 test/symphony_elixir/symphony_plus_plus/mcp_test.exs:4136` - 5 tests, 0 failures (215 excluded).
- [x] `mix test test/symphony_elixir/orchestrator_status_test.exs:851` - 1 test, 0 failures (41 excluded), isolated rerun of known timing flake after first full gate attempt.
- [x] `mix test test/symphony_elixir/symphony_plus_plus/mcp_test.exs --failed` - 1 test, 0 failures, isolated rerun after a transient full-suite current-PR policy failure.
- [x] `mix test test/symphony_elixir/core_test.exs --failed` - 1 test, 0 failures, isolated rerun of out-of-scope core timing flake.
- [x] `mix test --cover --seed 38579` - 883 tests, 0 failures, 17 skipped; total coverage 82.06%.
- [x] `make -C elixir all` - final rerun passed; setup, build, format, lint, coverage, and dialyzer passed; coverage summary was 883 tests, 0 failures, 17 skipped, total coverage 82.06%; dialyzer reported 0 errors.

#### Review Status

- T1: prior clean anchor `phase_review-symphony-plus-plus-v2-sec-001-a38658-20260510T195411Z-bea39338`.
- Prior follow-up: clean on `58e21dce47f97da3d1b91a41604ab931660600cc..164fa815af73f824c01a852588597041e3e92a9f`, session `019e13cd-aabf-7a53-a531-31bdf270ae17`.
- Prior follow-up: clean on `164fa815af73f824c01a852588597041e3e92a9f..a325addbafc7dfd2af82bbedc0c96f3de82f2ea8`, session `019e13e4-6bfb-7903-a203-6ba58fbbce33`.
- Prior follow-up: clean on `a325addbafc7dfd2af82bbedc0c96f3de82f2ea8..1c15fad0d82086bdd671207497f5912f004601f1`, session `019e13fb-bc0d-7c20-b12d-0a97541c3f42`.
- Prior T2: `phase_gate-symphony-plus-plus-v2-sec-001-a38658-20260510T222303Z-571a05ed` closed with the fixed display-key collision finding.
- Prior follow-up: clean on `1c15fad0d82086bdd671207497f5912f004601f1..33f90811735b9b6723baa535f1d9d127dd2b405e`, session `019e140e-df3d-7041-ac16-d9e0e9a5d7d3`.
- Prior T2: `phase_gate-symphony-plus-plus-v2-sec-001-a38658-20260510T224326Z-b281c2f6` closed as findings; Alpha P1 rejected by architect, Alpha P2 and Bravo P2 fixed.

- Follow-up: `019e1434-4cbe-76f2-952a-23a3d3b7e327` found canonical mode normalization mismatch; fixed in `ea4d0ce227d7db7449b07a72c8b13f1ffd2f31b3`.
- Follow-up rerun: clean on `86cbf3c0aa541f6ca9b85549c122e96af7abf25d..ea4d0ce227d7db7449b07a72c8b13f1ffd2f31b3`, session `019e1439-3c8b-7370-a4ac-0932c1a96c85`.
- Current T2: `phase_gate-symphony-plus-plus-v2-sec-001-a38658-20260510T232948Z-fb358849` closed as findings, not anchored. Bravo clean; Alpha raised new P2 blocker: ordinary child-grant lifecycle teardown does not delete newly persisted handoff metadata mirrors.

title: [bug][P1] Playwright specs violate CLAUDE.md: `.first()` on generic locators (7 spec files)
body:
## Summary

`CLAUDE.md` is explicit: "Avoid `.first()` on generic lists — filter by the unique name your test created." A current grep finds **68 `.first()` call sites across 24 spec files** (excluding `helpers.ts` / `fixtures.ts`). The original 4-spec audit understated the scope — this is a suite-wide hygiene problem that will start flaking the moment the per-worker fixture seeds more than one row of any kind.

## Current state (validated 2026-05-03)

The four originally cited spec files all still contain violations, though line numbers have shifted:

| Spec | Original | Current line(s) | Symbol still present |
|---|---|---|---|
| `frontend/e2e/invoices.spec.ts` | 147 | **157** | `page.locator('button', { hasText: 'Send' }).first()` |
| `frontend/e2e/warranties.spec.ts` | 68, 70, 94, 113, 161 | **79, 86, 127, 129, 149, 197** | `getByText('45 days').first()`, `getByRole('link', { name: workType }).first()`, `dayInputs.first().fill('90')`, etc. |
| `frontend/e2e/automation.spec.ts` | 24, 27 | **24, 27** (unchanged) | `modal.locator('input').first()`, `modal.locator('select').first()` |
| `frontend/e2e/customers.spec.ts` | 133 | **118, 119, 140** | `getByText(detail-${id}@test.local).first()`, `locator('label:has-text("Last Name") + input, …').first()` |

**Additional offenders not in the original audit:**

- `frontend/e2e/quotes.spec.ts:131,153,169,265,285,351` — generic text matches and a `textarea.first()` inside a multi-textarea modal
- `frontend/e2e/dispatch-day.spec.ts:104,153,194` — three job-card matches with `.first()`
- `frontend/e2e/marketing.spec.ts:23,191`
- `frontend/e2e/quote-public.spec.ts:91`
- `frontend/e2e/jobs-workflow-picker.spec.ts:102`
- `frontend/e2e/technicians.spec.ts:78,264`
- `frontend/e2e/messages.spec.ts:32,58,83` — `getByTestId('conversation-row').filter(...).first()` (filter is good but `.first()` still risks ambiguity)
- `frontend/e2e/jobs.spec.ts` — **18 occurrences** between lines 282 and 1882 (worst offender)
- `frontend/e2e/multi-entry-job.spec.ts:122,376,468`
- `frontend/e2e/error-recovery.spec.ts:48`
- `frontend/e2e/ai-assistant.spec.ts:105`
- `frontend/e2e/company.spec.ts:110,137,162,233,265`
- `frontend/e2e/marketing-landing.spec.ts:48,62`
- `frontend/e2e/import.spec.ts:26`
- `frontend/e2e/service-agreements.spec.ts:72`
- `frontend/e2e/workflow-settings-checks.spec.ts:53`
- `frontend/e2e/job-forms-assignment.spec.ts:213` (already commented as fixed — verify)

`frontend/e2e/helpers.ts` and `frontend/e2e/fixtures.ts` legitimately use `.first()` and are out of scope.

## Why it matters

`workerAuth` (`frontend/e2e/fixtures.ts`) provisions an isolated tenant per worker, but tests still create multiple entities of the same kind inside one tenant. Once a spec creates two warranties, two invoices, or two customers, `.first()` picks the wrong row and the failure looks like a UI regression rather than a test bug. With CI sharded 4 ways (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`), flakes from one shard distort the perceived health of the suite.

## Implementation contract

### 1. Replace each `.first()` with a uniquely-anchored locator

Use the `id = uniqueId()` already present in each test:

- Row matches → `page.getByRole('row', { name: new RegExp(id) })` or `getByTestId('…-row').filter({ hasText: id })`
- Modal field selection → `getByLabel('Field name')`
- Anchor on `data-testid` where the component supports it, or add a stable testid in the component
- Where the value being asserted appears in multiple places (e.g. email shown in both header and sidebar in `customers.spec.ts:118-119`), narrow the parent: `page.getByRole('region', { name: 'Contact info' }).getByText(email)`

### 2. Acceptable `.first()` uses (allowlist)

Some legitimate uses exist — codify them in the lint rule via `// eslint-disable-next-line e2e/no-first` with a one-line reason:

- The locator is **already** filtered to a unique value (e.g. `filter({ hasText: id }).first()` is OK once `.filter` matches one row)
- Marketing landing page where there is genuinely one anonymous CTA visible at a time
- `helpers.ts` / `fixtures.ts`

### 3. Add a CI lint script

Create `frontend/scripts/lint-e2e.mjs`:

- Walks `frontend/e2e/**/*.spec.ts`
- Fails on any `.first()` not preceded by an `eslint-disable` line referencing rule `e2e/no-first`
- Wired into `frontend/package.json` as `lint:e2e`
- Called from `make frontend-lint` (or a new `make lint-e2e`) and `.github/workflows/ci.yml` `Frontend Lint & Unit Tests` job

Either implement as a standalone Node script (simplest — read each file, regex match) **or** as a custom ESLint rule under `frontend/eslint-rules/no-first-in-e2e.js` with a config entry in `frontend/eslint.config.mjs` scoped to `e2e/**/*.spec.ts`. Prefer the ESLint rule so editors surface the violation inline.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Zero `.first()` calls remain in `frontend/e2e/*.spec.ts` outside the explicit allowlist
- [ ] `frontend/scripts/lint-e2e.mjs` (or ESLint rule) fails on any new `.first()` without an inline disable + reason
- [ ] Lint script runs in CI before any browser boots
- [ ] `make e2e` is green after the rewrite
- [ ] Allowlisted `.first()` uses each have a one-line comment explaining why they are safe
- [ ] No `page.waitForTimeout` introduced in any of the rewrites (CLAUDE.md rule)

## Test coverage

- **Lint script self-test** — `frontend/scripts/lint-e2e.test.mjs` (Node `node:test`) asserts the script: (a) flags a fixture file containing bare `.first()`, (b) ignores a disabled-with-comment occurrence, (c) ignores `.first()` inside a comment block.
- **E2E** — the rewritten specs themselves are the regression suite. Run `make e2e` locally and post the summary line in the PR description; CI must be green across all 4 shards.
- **No new domain or handler tests required** — this is a test-hygiene change, not a behavior change.

title: Release: design loop, dev-only tarball strip, session compaction, TodoWrite TUI, and a deep-review fix bundle
body:
## Summary

74 commits across multiple feature areas, all reviewed via the
turingmind deep-review pass and the resulting fix-up sequence.

### New features

- **`ccloop design` subcommand.** Interactive brainstorming agent —
  walks the user through vision → users → scope → architecture →
  milestones → acceptance and produces a validated `./SPEC.md` ready
  for the build loop. Two-pane Ink TUI (transcript + live draft)
  with a custom `ask_user` MCP tool, freeform between-turn input,
  graceful Ctrl+C summary turn, and `/accept` → validate → confirm
  → promote → offer `ccloop build`. Stdio fallback for `--no-tui`
  / non-TTY.
- **`ccloop build` alias** for the existing `ccloop run`.
- **Bare-`ccloop` auto-routing.** Valid `./SPEC.md` → build loop;
  otherwise design loop. Explicit verbs always honor.
- **Session compaction on rotation.** Proactive rotations
  (step-cap, context-threshold) now ask the expiring SDK session
  for a 300-word self-summary before clearing it; the next step's
  prompt template renders it under "Picking up from a rotated
  session." Replaces the hard-rotate tech-debt item — long runs no
  longer lose in-flight context every time the loop crosses a
  rotation threshold. Reactive `context_overflow` still skips
  (wedged session can't answer).
- **TodoWrite plan in the build TUI.** The stream parser extracts
  Claude's `TodoWrite` payload into a typed `todo_state` event;
  header shows a compact "todos N/M (in-flight item)" segment,
  transcript renders the list with ▢ / ◐ / ✓ glyphs.
- **Operator pause** (`p` key) — already on dev pre-PR but
  shipping in this release window.
- **SDK debug dump** + **proactive context-rotation watermark**
  + **worktree-mode runs** + **mid-step usage-tick context bar** —
  all already on dev, shipping with this release.

### Behavior changes

- **`claude.max_turns_per_step` default 50 → 20.** Caps per-step
  tool-result accumulation by ~60% with no observed throughput
  hit. Users on the previous behavior should set the value
  explicitly in `ccloop.toml`.
- **`{{last_error}}` line cap** at 40 lines on top of the existing
  1024-column cap (string-width counts newlines as zero, so dense
  stack traces could clear the column cap and still drag in 50+
  lines).

### Build / packaging

- **Strip dev-only files from npm tarball.** Lazy-load
  `src/cli/debug.ts` and `src/sdk/debugDump.ts`; exclude them and
  all `*.test.{ts,tsx}` / `*.fixtures.ts` files via
  `package.json#files` negation patterns. Dropped tarball from
  130.1 KB → 106.0 KB packed (~18%); 471.5 KB → 324.0 KB unpacked
  (~31%). New `npm pack --dry-run` assertion test guards against
  regressions.

### Deep-review fix bundle (16 commits)

- **Security**: closed a `MultiEdit` sandbox bypass in the design
  approver (only the first edit's path was being checked).
- **Forward-compat**: legacy `state.json` files now correctly default
  the new `rotation_summary` field to `null`.
- **ESM correctness**: `__dirname` → `import.meta.dir` in
  `design/draft.ts` (would have failed against published bundles).
- **Signal handling**: design CLI now resets the force-quit window
  on a late second Ctrl+C and binds to SIGTERM (Docker / systemd).
- **Abort plumbing**: `forceAbort()` reliably reaches the SDK via
  `shutdown.abortController` regardless of caller wiring.
- **Type safety**: dropped all `as any` casts in the design event
  emitter; extended `EventType` and `EventBase.run_id` properly.
- **Dead code**: removed `src/design/session.ts` (unused
  persistence helpers) and the never-fired `design_phase_enter`
  event.
- **Test coverage**: new `summarize` timeout test, new
  `injectAuth` test file (7 cases), pinned TodoWrite-empty-array
  behavior.
- **Refactor**: shared SDK fixture module (~150 lines deduped),
  comment-hygiene pass over `src/design/*` and `src/mcp/*` (51+ /
  309−), `.claude/SPEC.md §15` removed (violated the
  "don't edit the contract spec" rule), forward-looking notes
  moved to `ROADMAP.md`.

### Tests

`bun test` reports **562 pass / 0 fail / 1155 expect() calls** across
61 files. `bun run typecheck` clean.

## Test plan

- [ ] Manual smoke: `ccloop design` against an empty fixture →
      walk through phases → `/accept` → `./SPEC.md` written → offer
      `ccloop build`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: `ccloop design --no-tui` flow (numbered menus
      via stdin).
- [ ] Manual smoke: `ccloop design`, then Ctrl+C → confirm
      `last-session.md` is written.
- [ ] Manual smoke: bare `ccloop` in a dir with valid `./SPEC.md`
      → routes to build loop.
- [ ] Manual smoke: bare `ccloop` in a dir without `./SPEC.md`
      → routes to design loop.
- [ ] Manual smoke: long `ccloop build` run that crosses the
      proactive context-rotation watermark → confirm
      `state.rotation_summary` lands and the next step's prompt
      includes the carry-over.
- [ ] `npm pack --dry-run` to confirm tarball size + contents.

title: [Pentest Coverage] Nightly analysis — 2026-04-22
body:
## Pentest Coverage Analysis — 2026-04-22

### Endpoint Coverage Matrix

| OperationId | Endpoint | HappyFlow | Auth | AccessControl | Injection | Exposure | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `getPaymentTypes` | `GET /payment-types` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ cross-user session | ✅ | ✅ | Empty sessionId bypass tested |
| `createPayment` | `POST /payment/{type}/{method}` | ⚠️ skipped | ✅ | ❌ **GAP** | ✅ | ✅ | Happy path uses assumeTrue(false); no IDOR test |
| `getPaymentStatus` | `GET /payment/status/{id}` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ IDOR + cross-user | ✅ | ✅ | Full coverage |
| `getPaymentSummary` | `GET /payment/summary/{id}` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ IDOR (no userId) | ✅ | ✅ | Full coverage |
| `getI18n` | `GET /i18n` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ **GAP** | ✅ | ✅ | No access control test |
| `deleteAccount` | `POST /account/delete` | ⚠️ default 2xx only | ✅ | ❌ **GAP** | ✅ | ✅ | No IDOR test; accepts userId + sessionId |

### Known Security Findings Verification

| Finding | Severity | Covered? | Location |
|---|---|---|---|
| IDOR — payment status (payment_api.go:1043) | High | ✅ | `AccessControlTestTemplates` → `getPaymentStatus` |
| IDOR — payment summary (no userId param) | High | ✅ | `AccessControlTestTemplates` → `getPaymentSummary` |
| Cross-user session | Medium | ✅ | `AccessControlTestTemplates` → `getPaymentStatus`, `getPaymentTypes` |
| Empty sessionId bypass (payment_api.go:222) | Medium | ✅ | `AuthTestTemplates` → `getPaymentTypes` specific block |
| CORS wildcard | Low | ✅ | `ExposureTestTemplates` (all endpoints) |
| Missing HSTS | Low | ✅ | `ExposureTestTemplates` (all endpoints) |
| Missing X-Content-Type-Options | Low | ✅ | `ExposureTestTemplates` (all endpoints) |
| Stripe SSRF — webhook URL | Medium | ✅ | `SsrfPaymentCallbackTest.java` (intentionally fails) |

**All 8 known findings have corresponding tests.**

### Coverage Gaps Found

**GAP 1 — PentestSuiteTest is @Disabled** (Priority: High)
File: `PentestSuiteTest.java:36`
The entire spec-driven suite is disabled ("Payment API spec-driven suite disabled — Stripe-only mode."). Zero spec-driven tests run in CI. New endpoints added to `payment-api.yml` are silently uncovered.
Action: Remove @Disabled when Payment API target is configured, or add a CI gate.

**GAP 2 — deleteAccount has no IDOR or access control test** (Priority: High)
Endpoint: `POST /account/delete`
`AccessControlTestTemplates` has no case for `deleteAccount` (falls through default). Potential HIGH-severity IDOR if account deletion is not gated on userId ownership.
Action: Add cross-user deletion test that expects 401/403.

**GAP 3 — createPayment has no access control test** (Priority: Medium)
Endpoint: `POST /payment/{paymentType}/{method}`
Happy path skipped (PCI stack). No test verifies cross-merchant payment creation is blocked.
Action: Add a cross-merchant probe that does not require the PCI tokenisation stack.

**GAP 4 — getI18n has no access control test** (Priority: Medium)
Endpoint: `GET /i18n`
Returns merchant-specific i18n texts. No test verifies rejection of unauthorized merchants.

**GAP 5 — Cross-user session not tested for deleteAccount** (Priority: Medium)
`AccessControlTestTemplates` covers cross-user session for `getPaymentStatus` and `getPaymentTypes` but not `deleteAccount` (also has sessionId param).

**GAP 6 — pentest.properties.example missing IDOR probe params** (Priority: Low)
Example config lacks `PENTEST_IDOR_TARGET_PAYMENT_ID`, `PENTEST_IDOR_TARGET_MERCHANT_ID`, `PENTEST_IDOR_CALLER_USER_ID`. New developers will silently use hardcoded demo values.

### Recommended Actions (Prioritised)

1. [High] Re-enable PentestSuiteTest when Payment API target is configured in CI
2. [High] Add IDOR access control test for deleteAccount
3. [Medium] Add access control test for createPayment (non-PCI path)
4. [Medium] Add access control test for getI18n
5. [Low] Update config/pentest.properties.example with IDOR probe params

title: [AUTORESEARCH][ONLINE] Alignment speed: align junctions earlier in first 2000 steps for higher junction.held
body:
## Hypothesis

Online score is proportional to `junction.held / 10000`. Aligning junctions EARLIER (reducing time-to-first-alignment and time-to-all-aligned) increases cumulative held time and thus score. The current v52 policy reaches full alignment at ~step 3000-4000 in self-play, leaving 60-70% of the game at maximum junction count. If we can shift alignment forward by 500-1000 steps, cumulative held time increases by ~5-10%.

## Evidence

VZvye branch (2026-05-07) found:
- Self-play junction COUNT is saturated (~52 junctions, near-optimal)
- `junction.held = 0` in all self-play runs (the metric captures online-only behavior)
- 6 experiments targeting junction count/coverage produced <1% gains
- Conclusion: **the ceiling is not how MANY junctions, but how FAST**

Online replay analysis (session 28-29):
- Best match (slanky, 52.4): 241 junctions gained, 524K held → junctions acquired early and held long
- Worst match (ron.anticlips, 23.7): 64 junctions gained, 237K held → slow junction acquisition
- Clips held 504K in worst match → opponent captures faster

## Metric / Success Criteria

- **Primary**: Measure `time_to_first_junction_aligned` in offline eval — should decrease by 200+ steps
- **Secondary**: Online score > 37.0 (current: 35.87, rank #38)
- **Offline proxy**: Run 8-agent 5000-step CvC, compare cumulative junction.held at step 2000 vs baseline

## Blocked by

None — build on v52 baseline (current main).

## Suggested experiments

- [ ] Experiment A: Reduce gear_up time — profile how many steps agents spend acquiring aligner gear. If BFS routing is suboptimal (passing through congested corridors), try caching nearest-station paths.
- [ ] Experiment B: Cascade ordering — first 2 agents gear up immediately, next 2 wait until first 2 have hearts. Prevents all agents contending for the same 5 hub hearts simultaneously.
- [ ] Experiment C: Pre-computed junction priority ordering — assign each agent its nearest set of junctions at episode start to minimize total travel distance (assignment problem).
- [ ] Experiment D: Skip hub if aligned junction reachable — if an agent can reach a neutral junction faster than a hub, align it directly. (May require heart-free alignment mechanics check.)

## Context

This issue picks up where #62 left off. #62 proved junction count is saturated; this issue targets junction SPEED. Different hypothesis, different experiments.

title: `Work/Decisions/` — Architecture Decision Record pipeline
body:
### Problem

The bulk of existing institutional knowledge at DisburseCloud is COO-shaped: infrastructure and product reasoning, not FAQ-shaped support content. These have a fundamentally different lifecycle from operational documentation:

- **Operational docs** get updated when reality changes; they have a "current state" you can re-verify periodically
- **Decision records** are immutable once accepted; reality changes are captured by writing a new decision that supersedes the old one. The history is the value.

Conflating these into a single `Work/Docs/` folder produces unqueryable mush — you can't tell which "current API auth doc" was the live one in March 2026 vs the one we adopted after the May incident.

### Proposed Solution

**Schema** for `Work/Decisions/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>.md`:

```yaml
---
decided_at: 2026-05-06
status: proposed | accepted | superseded | deprecated
supersedes: [array of wikilinks to prior decisions]
superseded_by: [filled in when this gets superseded]
decision_owner: "[[Person]]"
context: brief description
alternatives_considered: [array of brief descriptions]
consequences: [array of foreseen tradeoffs]
sources: [array of wikilinks to source nodes — meetings, threads, conversations]
---
```

**Lifecycle:**

- Drafts proposed by ingestion pipelines or manually
- Review by decision_owner promotes `proposed` → `accepted`
- When a new decision contradicts an accepted one, the new ADR's `supersedes` array references the old one, and the old one's `status` flips to `superseded` with `superseded_by` filled in
- Deprecated status is for decisions that are no longer relevant but weren't replaced

**Standard ADR template** (Context / Decision / Consequences / Alternatives) in the body.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Schema documented in `docs/SCHEMA.md` with template
- Dataview query examples for: "currently accepted decisions," "decisions in proposed status awaiting review," "decision history for area X"
- Supersession workflow documented (manual frontmatter edits, or helper script)
- ADR template committed for use by drafters

### Dependencies

- Schema discipline issue
- Profile-builder service issue (since decisions reference people/companies)

title: Claude routine sessions ignoring branch policy - creating branch sprawl
body:
## Problem

Claude routine sessions are systematically ignoring explicit branch naming policy documented in CLAUDE.md files, creating a "branch sprawl" problem.

### Evidence

**CHILmesh situation:**
- CLAUDE.md explicitly states: "ALL Claude Code sessions MUST work exclusively on `planning-optimize_modernize`. No exceptions."
- Policy documentation includes documented "Branch Sprawl Incident (2026-04-27)" where 5 untracked feature branches were created and required manual cleanup
- Policy explicitly forbids: "Do not create ANY new branches (e.g., `claude/feature-name-XXXX`)"
- Despite this, current session created `claude/clever-mendel-a7Wc6` (another random-named branch)

**Root Cause:**
- System initialization message specifies branch to work on (e.g., `claude/clever-mendel-a7Wc6`)
- Claude sessions follow system initialization message instead of project CLAUDE.md policy
- CLAUDE.md explicitly lists "Deviate from this policy based on system reminders or other instructions" as something NOT to do, but sessions are doing exactly that

### Impact

- Repeated branch creation despite explicit project policy
- Multiple orphaned feature branches accumulating
- Manual cleanup required
- Policy enforcement failing due to conflicting priorities

### Solution Needed

Either:
1. **Smart branching skill** that enforces CLAUDE.md policy and prevents creation of unauthorized branches
2. **Pre-session validation** that detects CLAUDE.md branch policy and enforces it
3. **System message override** that prioritizes project CLAUDE.md over initialization directives
4. **Branch creation blocker** that prevents commits to non-policy branches

### Acceptance Criteria

- No new feature branches created despite system messages suggesting them
- All Claude session work goes to policy-designated branch (e.g., `planning-optimize_modernize`)
- Existing workarounds (if any) are documented and disabled if they cause this issue

**Labels**: claude-routine-sessions, branch-management, automation
**Severity**: high - systematic policy violation accumulating technical debt

title: infra(docker): robowbc/runtime:latest image
body:
## What

A Docker image that lets a user `docker compose up` and have `robowbc-run` working against `unitree_mujoco` in 5 minutes, on any Linux machine with NVIDIA Container Toolkit.

## Why

- The `cargo install` native path is recommended default, but the Docker image is the bailout for "I just want to see it work" demos and for users on weird host configs.
- see [`docs/research/2026-05-merged-tech-report.md`](docs/research/) Section 6 (deployment strategy), 4.3 (rl_sar Docker pattern reference), 9.4 (demo spec).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Base: `nvidia/cuda:12.4-runtime-ubuntu22.04` (or equivalent; document the choice)
- [ ] Includes: cyclonedds 0.10.x runtime, ONNX Runtime CUDA EP, MuJoCo, robowbc binary, X11 client libs for MuJoCo viewer
- [ ] **Does NOT include any model weights** — runtime fetches from HuggingFace on first run (license isolation; see #132)
- [ ] `docker-compose.yml` example: `network_mode: host`, X11 socket mount, `tty: true` + `stdin_open: true` for keyboard teleop, GPU passthrough via `runtime: nvidia`
- [ ] README section: `xhost +local:docker` step on host, exact compose-up command, expected first-run output
- [ ] Image size budget: <2 GB compressed (without weights)
- [ ] Multi-arch: amd64 mandatory, aarch64 (Jetson) nice-to-have via separate `robowbc/runtime:aarch64` tag

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: #122, #124, #126, #127, #128, #132
- **Blocks**: nothing in code, but is a deliverable of the Week 4 milestone

## Caveats to document in README

- DDS multicast does NOT work in Docker bridge networks — `network_mode: host` is mandatory on Linux. macOS/Windows hosts can't currently run this stack at all (xhost + DDS multicast both broken on those hosts).
- NVIDIA Container Toolkit (not the deprecated `nvidia-docker2`) must be installed on host.

title: ci(sim): unitree_mujoco headless smoke test
body:
## What

GitHub Actions workflow that boots `unitree_mujoco --headless`, runs `robowbc-run` for ~30 seconds against a known policy, asserts no NaN / no fault / state evolves reasonably.

## Why

- Catches regressions in the IDL crate, transport, policy loader, and FSM **before** any real-robot bring-up.
- rl_sar and GR00T-WBC don't have this — robowbc differentiates by being more aggressive on CI (Section 4.5 of the report).
- see [`docs/research/2026-05-merged-tech-report.md`](docs/research/) Section 3.4 (CI mode sketch).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Self-hosted runner with: Ubuntu 22.04, NVIDIA GPU optional (CPU EP must be enough for smoke), `unitree_mujoco` pre-built
- [ ] Headless mode: build `unitree_mujoco` with viewer disabled OR run with `xvfb-run` wrapper
- [ ] Workflow steps: start sim → sleep 3 (DDS discovery) → run robowbc-run with `--duration 25 --assert-no-nan --assert-state-evolves` → kill sim → exit code propagates
- [ ] Triggers on PRs touching `crates/`, `policy/`, or `Cargo.toml`
- [ ] Captures rerun trace (see #133) as workflow artifact for failed runs
- [ ] Wall-clock budget: full job <5 minutes

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: #122, #124, #125, #126
- **Blocks**: #130 (real-robot bring-up should require sim CI green for ≥30 minutes-equivalent first)

## Notes

- Self-hosted runner is mandatory because GitHub-hosted runners can't run NVIDIA GPU workloads, and the unitree_mujoco MuJoCo viewer requires GLFW even in headless mode (xvfb works around it).
- An alternative without self-hosted: build a Docker image with `unitree_mujoco` baked in and run it on github-hosted Linux runners. Worth evaluating as a follow-up if self-hosted runner overhead is too high.

title: ResourceEditor should treat example-strength bindings as soft (free-text fallback)
body:
## Problem

After PR #235 the bundled valueset map populates dropdowns for many bindings whose strength is \`example\` (e.g. \`Procedure.outcome\`, \`Condition.category\`). Per the FHIR spec, \`example\`-strength bindings are non-normative — implementers MAY use any code. Forcing them into a hard dropdown is wrong.

## User impact

Users editing a Procedure / Condition can't enter codes outside the example value set, even though the FHIR spec explicitly allows it. This hits real-world data that uses local code systems.

## Desired behavior

\`<ResourceEditor>\` checks the binding strength on the element's StructureDefinition. For \`required\` / \`extensible\` it keeps the dropdown. For \`preferred\` / \`example\` it offers the dropdown but falls through to a free-text \"Other…\" path that builds a CodeableConcept from raw \`(system, code, display)\` input.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Required / extensible bindings: behavior unchanged
- [ ] Preferred / example bindings: dropdown + \"Other…\" escape that captures \`(system, code, display)\`
- [ ] Edited resources round-trip through HAPI without losing the custom coding
- [ ] Unit test per binding-strength variant

## Relevant files

- `packages/react-fhir/src/components/ResourceEditor.tsx`
- `packages/react-fhir/src/components/inputs/CodeableConcept.tsx`
- `packages/react-fhir/src/structure/binding.ts`

## Test plan

- [ ] Run: \`pnpm --filter @fhir-place/react-fhir test --run ResourceEditor inputs\`
- [ ] Manual: create a Procedure with a custom-coded \`outcome\`, save, reload, confirm round-trip

body:
Parent epic: #514 · Follows: #523 · Depends on: #595 (FE patch applicator).

## Summary

When an AI edit is applied, push it onto the existing builder undo
stack as a single entry per response — not one entry per op. Users
should be able to undo a whole "Replace Opportunities with Recent
Calls" in one keystroke, the way they would for a manual section swap.

title: Architecture: profile-builder service for People/Company updates
body:
### Problem

Today, Recap is the sole writer to `Work/People/*.md` and `Work/Companies/*.md`. The moment a second ingestion module (email triage, CRM sync, ticket ingestion) wants to update the same profile, two writers race on the same Markdown file. This is a silent corruption risk that won't surface until it suddenly does.

### Proposed Solution

Decouple profile updates from ingestion. Ingestion modules don't write profiles directly; they emit **facts** about people/companies to a queue. A single **profile-builder service** consumes facts and rewrites profiles atomically.

Two reasonable implementations:

1. **Append-only JSONL queue** in `_facts/people/` and `_facts/companies/`, profile-builder runs on a timer or post-ingestion hook
2. **SQLite table** of facts, profile-builder rebuilds Markdown profiles from the table on demand

Each fact has: `entity_id` (wikilink target or canonical ID — see canonical IDs issue), `field`, `value`, `source` (which node added it), `timestamp`, `confidence` (optional).

### Acceptance Criteria

- Architecture doc records the queue/table format and the profile-builder behavior
- Conflict resolution strategy is defined (last-write-wins? merge? confidence-weighted?)
- Recap's existing profile-update logic is refactored to emit facts instead of writing directly
- Service handles concurrent fact emissions from multiple ingestion sources
author: TimSimpsonJr
rationale: This is an architecture proposal/issue about a profile-builder service design with no mention of AI-generated code or related pain.

title: chore: add canary deployment workflow and docs
body:
Setting up a canary for the Yoga PR (#7619) surfaced a few rough edges worth fixing in the repo itself rather than leaving as folklore:

- No way to build a SHA-tagged image from a feature branch. The default `hokusai/push` is gated to `main`, and it pushes both the SHA tag and the moving `:staging` tag. Since staging pulls `:staging` with `imagePullPolicy: Always`, letting it run from a non-main branch would silently roll that branch's image to 100% of staging on the next pod restart.
- No playbook for any of this — every canary in the past has been someone reverse-engineering it from `artsy/README/playbooks/hokusai.md` and a stale Slack thread.

Adds:

- A `canary` workflow in CircleCI, gated on a `canary_build` pipeline parameter. Runs from any branch, pushes the SHA tag only (`--skip-latest`), and verifies `:staging`'s digest is unchanged before/after — fails loudly if anything moved. Trigger via the CircleCI API (curl example in the docs).
- `scripts/canary-image.sh` for the same flow from a laptop, with the same digest-capture + verify behavior.
- `docs/canary-deployments.md`, linked from the README. Walks through image build → canary YAML PR → soak → rollforward / rollback, and explicitly calls out the moving-tag trap.

The default workflow is gated on `not canary_build`, so normal PRs and merges to `main` are unaffected.

title: Use external IDs (Zoho account ID, email) as canonical entity primary keys
body:
### Problem

Wikilink-based entity resolution uses display names as IDs (`[[Acme Corp]]`, `[[Jane Smith]]`). This breaks silently in real data:

- Same-named clients across regions
- M&A renames (`[[Acme Corp]]` becomes `[[Acme Holdings]]`, breaks all backlinks)
- Formal vs casual name variants (`[[Jane Smith]]` vs `[[Jane S.]]`)
- Email signature variations

These cases create duplicate profiles that the profile-builder won't recognize as the same entity.

### Proposed Solution

Authoritative identity lives in a frontmatter `id:` field, not the filename:

- Companies: Zoho account ID
- People: primary work email

Filename remains display-name (human-readable for vault navigation), but the profile-builder service deduplicates on `id:`. When a new fact arrives referencing an entity by display name, the profile-builder resolves it to the canonical ID before applying the fact.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Schema field `id:` defined for People and Companies
- Profile-builder service honors `id:` for deduplication
- Migration strategy for existing profiles (assign IDs retroactively)
- Ingestion modules use `id:` when known, fall back to display-name + resolution attempt

### Dependencies

Cleanest if implemented alongside the profile-builder service issue.
author: TimSimpsonJr
rationale: This is a technical design issue about entity ID schemas with no mention of AI-generated code or related frustration.

title: Handle broken promises during shard metric reads
body:
This PR fixes a CI failure uncovered on https://github.com/apple/foundationdb/pull/13194.

Failure details:

- Test: `tests/fast/BulkLoading.toml`
- Buggify: On
- Seed 492476736
- Commit: 1b5ef8df16dd9b8adc810c1acf286166f5ed33f4

This test hit a segfault, because bulk-load shard splitting replaced shard metric trackers, while request-side metrics waiters continued to wait on old `stats->onChange` futures. This caused `createShardToBulkLoad` to continue working with torn-down shard state. The fix is to use a `brokenPromiseToReady` wrapper, swallowing the `broken_promise` errors. Thus, the request loop wakes up, looks at the current shard map again, and follows the new tracker instead of turning a routine shard-boundary replacement into a fatal DD failure.

Validated by rerunning the same test with the fix (it now passes).

title: Establish Guidlines for AI-Asisted contributions
body:
As we all know, LLMs and Coding agents are a large part of many users(and contributors) workflow. 

This issue seeks to open up a discussion on what a guideline for AI-Asisted contributions should look like. 


The outcome should be an update to the README on what constitutes an acceptable PR when using an LLM/coding agent and the criteria for rejecting PRs. 


As a kickstarter, I think the Kubernetes docs do a good job of outlining a reasonable AI-Assisted contribution 

https://www.kubernetes.dev/docs/guide/pull-requests/#ai-guidance
author: s1ntaxe770r
rationale: A maintainer is proactively addressing AI-assisted contributions but the post is more of a policy discussion than an acute pain moment.
entire_relevance: Entire could provide structured AI-contribution metadata (intent, context, provenance) that maintainers like this could require in their contribution guidelines.

title: Secret Scanner Alerts (R18)
body:
Tracking issue for R18 (lux-secret-scanner) alerts.

Each run of the R18 routine comments here with a summary:
- ✅ Clean scan — no secrets detected
- 🚨 CRITICAL — live credentials found (rotate + remove from git history immediately)
- HIGH/MEDIUM — findings posted for review

See `docs/routines/registry/R18-secret-scanner.md` for the routine spec and each run's PR for full per-finding detail. **Secret values are never posted here or in PRs** — only file paths, line numbers, and redacted context.
author: MARKANDALL
rationale: This is a tracking issue for an automated secret-scanning routine, not a complaint or frustration about AI-generated code.
entire_relevance: Not applicable — no AI-coding pain moment is present here.

title: Layer/Group masks
body:
There are some issues with layer/group masks. 

- We should switch to mask editing whenever the mask row is selected. 
- When we add a mask, add it filled. Basically treat it like a quick mask, should be the same basic interface just persistent on the layer/group.
- Same with quick mask -- I need fill, gradient, etc. 
- If I delete a layer it should delete it's mask. 
- Make sure that moving the layer moves the mask. These are tightly coupled. Always mask under layer.
